1998-01-01.title: French Police Press the Search for a Fiat in Diana Crash
1998-01-01.abstract: French investigators continue their search for white Fiat that may have brushed Mercedes carrying Princess Diana, just before crash that killed her and two others; authorities say they still believe that excessive drink and high speed were what made driver, Henri Paul, lose control of car (M)

1998-01-01.title: Kenya's President Takes Lead as Ballot Counting Drags On
1998-01-01.abstract: Counting of ballots in Kenya's general election drags on, as more reports of irregularities and violent incidents at counting stations add to questions about fairness of polls; both sides appear to be setting stage to challenge results, raising fears of political violence; Pres Daniel arap Moi, who is seeking fifth term, appears to take early lead (M)

1998-01-01.title: Na Tao Journal; Asia's Wealth Ebbs, but Laos Is Too Poor to Care
1998-01-01.abstract: Laos, one of 10 poorest countries in world, is landlocked Communist nation of 4.6 million people; its single annual rice crop, already too small for its needs, is devastated almost every year by natural disasters; chronic malnutrition stunts growth of nearly half the children; fewer than half the adults in Laos can read; but average annual income, small as it still is, has nearly doubled since Government opened country's economy to foreign investment and free enterprise, and even poorest villager can now get taste of outside world by viewing programs from abroad on battery-operated televisions; photo; map (M)

1998-01-01.title: U.S. General Says Conflict With Iraqis Is Still Possible
1998-01-01.abstract: Gen Anthony C Zinni, commander of American forces in Persian Gulf, says American military buildup probably thwarted threat of hostilities by Iraq, but warns that military confrontation remains possible; says he does not know when US will be able to withdraw additional forces sent to region last month during lastest showdown with Iraq (M)

1998-01-01.title: State Crimes Shake Turkey As Politicians Face Charges
1998-01-01.abstract: Turkish court rules that Sedat Bucak and Mehmet Agar, leading figures in major political scandal, may be tried for crimes they claim they committeed on behalf of the state; two were named in parliamentary report investigating charges that successive Turkish Governments have used death squads against Kurdish nationalists and other perceived enemies; defendants do not deny anti-terrorist work, but say it was authorized by high officials; scandal erupted after 1996 crash of car in which Bucak, who heads pro-Government Kurdish militia, was riding with senior police officer and convicted heroin smuggler wanted by police and Interpol (M)

1998-01-02.title: Spate of Killings Puts Cloud Over Talks on Ulster Peace
1998-01-02.abstract: Protestant paramilitaries attack pub in Catholic area of Belfast, killing one man and wounding five others and threatening fragile Northern Ireland peace talks; slaying is third sectarian killing in province in week; photo (M)

1998-01-02.title: U.S. Used Special Authority to Admit Cuban Ballplayers
1998-01-02.abstract: Atty Gen Janet Reno's decision to invoke rarely used authority to clear Cuban baseball star Orlando Hernandez, his wife and baseball player Alberto Hernandez followed lengthy Clinton Administration debate; did not extend to five other Cubans who fled with them and were stranded on Bahamian island; Administration aides say ballplayers face certain persecution if returned to Cuba and would have qualified for special visas were they from any other nation (M)

1998-01-02.title: Government to Ask for Donations of Gold Jewelry : Self-Help Glitters in Thailand
1998-01-02.abstract: BANGKOK—  Faced with the cold reality that foreign investors will not return anytime soon, the Thai government will ask Thais next week to hand in their gold jewelry to help bail out their nation, according to the country's finance minister.

1998-01-02.title: Killings in Ulster Jar Planned Peace Talks
1998-01-02.abstract: Tensions over the future of the fragile peace talks in Northern Ireland grew yesterday after Protestant paramilitaries killed a Catholic man and wounded five others in a pub on New Year's Eve. It was the third sectarian killing in the province in a week.    At the Clifton Tavern in North Belfast, two men, one carrying a handgun and the other a submachine gun, fired at random, then sped away in a waiting car with a woman in the back yelling in triumph, according to onlookers.

1998-01-02.title: Kenya's President Holds Lead in Tense Race
1998-01-02.abstract: Ballot counting continues in Kenya's presidential race, with ex-Vice Pres Mwai Kibaki trailing Pres Daniel arap Moi by small margin; situation remains explosive, with violence possible by loser's supporters and both men accusing electoral commission of rigging race; Kibai urges supporters to remain calm; commits self to forming national unity government with Raila Odinga and other opposition leaders (M)

1998-01-03.title: World News Briefs; Solo American Balloonist Races Across Atlantic
1998-01-03.abstract: Steve Fossett's Solo Spirit balloon, racing along at up to 161 miles per hour, reaches Europe barely two days after ascending from St Louis (S)

1998-01-03.title: Toledo Journal; After 5 Centuries, a Mecca for Translators Again
1998-01-03.abstract: Toledo School of Translators in Toledo, Spain, brings together writers and translators from around the Mediterranean in the hope of making Europe better acquainted with cultures of immigrant people, many of them Muslims from North Africa; harks back to Toledo School of 13th and 14th centuries, where scholars translated Hebrew and Arabic writings on medicine, philosophy, matematics, astronomy and botany into Latin and the languages of Europe; new school focuses for the moment on Arabic and Hebrew writing, but there are plans to include works of writers from Turkey and Albania; photo (M)

1998-01-03.title: Suspense Is Rising in Israel Over Impasse on the Budget
1998-01-03.abstract: Suspense continues in Israel about whether Foreign Min David Levy will carry out threat to resign from Cabinet; stalemate reported in talks with Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu on Levy's demands for more social spending; Netanyahu postpones budget vote (S)

1998-01-03.title: Kenya's President Headed For Victory in Chaotic Vote
1998-01-03.abstract: Unofficial tallies show Pres Daniel arap Moi of Kenya headed toward re-election, but election is flawed and chaotic; two top opposition candidates, Mwai Kibaki and Raila Odinga, call on Electoral Commission to cancel results, which they say were skewed by widespread ballot shortages, delays and outright fraud in some cases; accuse governing party of rigging elections; photo (M)

1998-01-03.title: J.G. Williams, 84, a Legend As Naturalist And Guide
1998-01-03.abstract: John George Williams, self-taught naturalist who gained international renown as safari guide to bird life and other fauna of eastern and central Africa, dies at 84 (M)

1998-01-04.title: American Weaver Revives Old Laotian Art
1998-01-04.abstract: American entrepreneur Carol Cassidy is trying to revive Laotian art of silk weaving; she started her company, Lao Textiles, in 1990; it was first American company licensed in Vientiane after end of Indochina war in 1975, and is still one of few Western businesses in country; her business is thriving at time when Laotian textile industry has been badly wounded by recently imposed foreign tariffs; Cassidy pays her weavers professional wages, which can reach to nearly three times average national income of $350 a year; map; photo (M)

1998-01-04.title: Mexico's Interior Minister Is Replaced in Aftermath of Massacre
1998-01-04.abstract: Mexican Pres Ernesto Zedillo dismisses Interior Min Emilio Chuayffet in aftermath of massacre in Chiapas, and names Francisco Labastida Ochoa to succeed him (M)

1998-01-04.title: Russia's Church-Run Campus Has a Secular Goal
1998-01-04.abstract: Russian Orthodox Church has founded university in Moscow that offers mainstream education; Russian Orthodox University of St John the Theologian is only church-run university in country that is training young people for professional world of new Russia; photo (M)

1998-01-04.title: U.N. Discord Is Music to Iraq and a Threat To Security Council Credibility.
1998-01-04.abstract: Barbara Crossette analysis contends that with United Nations' chief weapons inspector, Richard Butler, going to Iraq in two weeks for another round of negotiations over Baghdad's refusal to allow full access to suspect sites, Security Council appears to be more paralyzed than it has been on any other issue since end of cold war (M)

1998-01-04.title: Unlikely Twist: G.I.'s Warm to Bosnia
1998-01-04.abstract: Many of 8,500 American soldiers in Bosnia have gone from dispassionate observers in confusing land to soldiers who feel their job is to help people they now know and like; map; photo (M)

1998-01-05.title: Beijing Journal; Art's Fate In China: A Battle By an Heir
1998-01-05.abstract: Late Qi Baishi is one of China's best-known 20th-century painters; his grandson, Qi Bingyi, hopes to preserve painter's Beijing house as museum; he wants artworks that were seized during Cultural Revolution returned; photo (M)

1998-01-05.title: Korea Reported To Seek Billions In Bond Offers
1998-01-05.abstract: South Korean Government is attempting on its own to secure as much as $35 billion in new financing, according to American commercial and investment bankers; move comes as Bank for International Settlements releases report pointing out that global bankers stepped up lending to country in early 1997 despite indications of 'growing strains in Southeast Asia'; South Korea, by moving to sell bonds on its own, will sidestep American commercial banks' efforts to lead repackaging of country's mountain of troubled loans; almost $40 billion in short-term South Korean debt will have to be restructured by end of March to ease crisis (M)

1998-01-05.title: NETANYAHU'S HOLD ON POWER IS HURT AS MINISTER QUITS
1998-01-05.abstract: David Levy resigns as Israel's Foreign Minister, saying he has no further place in Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu's right-wing coalition; without five votes of Levy's faction, Netanyahu has only 61-59 majority in Parliament; Levy has criticized Netanyahu for lack of money for social welfare programs in 1998 budget and stagnation in peace negotiations with Palestinians; photos (M)

1998-01-05.title: Cambodia Coup Leader Battles Instability
1998-01-05.abstract: Hun Sen, who six months ago led coup in Cambodia in which he ousted his coalition partner, Norodom Ranariddh, is still struggling to consolidate political and military control in country; he has failed to win international legitimacy as criticism continues from abroad about his Government's human rights abuses and repression; political, military and international pressures on Hun Sen have begun to intensify with election expected later this year; photo (M)

1998-01-05.title: Speculation About Pol Pot Stirs Cambodia's Uncertainty
1998-01-05.abstract: Unconfirmed reports are circulating in Thailand and Cambodia that Khmer Rouge founder, Pol Pot, may have fled his jungle stronghold in that country and taken refuge in China (M)

1998-01-06.title: German Churches, Ever Giving, Ask to Receive
1998-01-06.abstract: Many would-be seminarians in Germany are finding careers derailed because of economic problems that have caused hardships for state-financed religion; religious institutions get almost all their revenue from 9 percent church surtax imposed on income tax of every registered Catholic, Protestant and Jew, and tax revenues have plummeted since 1996 amid rising unemployment that has forced many Germans to stop paying surtax; map; photo (M)

1998-01-06.title: French Unemployed Turn Their Ire on the Socialists
1998-01-06.abstract: France's Socialist Government, which was elected on pledge to alleviate stubborn 12.4 percent unemployment rate, has been unable to keep promise and has emerged from holiday season on defense against jobless themselves; some of 3.1 million unemployed have occupied more than dozen unemployment offices nationwide, demanding raises in unemployment benefits and New Year's bonuses, just like people with jobs (M)

1998-01-06.title: Palestinians Protest to CBS News Over a Report on the West Bank
1998-01-06.abstract: Palestinian Authority bars CBS News from access to officials or territories under its control to protest report on 60 Minutes news program on December 7 which allegedly failed to follow up on information provided by Authority about supposed complicity of Israeli officials in monopolies controlling sale of certain goods in parts of West Bank under Palestinian control (S)

1998-01-06.title: U.S. Balloonist Falls About Three-Quarters of a Globe Short
1998-01-06.abstract: Steve Fossett abandons quest to become first balloonist to circumnavigate globe nonstop; is forced down in wheatfield in Krasnodar, Russia, after 7,300 miles because of unfavorable winds, broken control for gas burner and malfunctioning heater that left him shivering 27,000 feet above ground; map; photo (M)

1998-01-06.title: Sworn for 5th Term, Kenya's President Vows to Fight Corruption and Poverty
1998-01-06.abstract: Daniel arap Moi is sworn in for 5th term as President of Kenya; vows to do better job tackling extensive official corruption and endemic poverty (M)

1998-01-07.title: After Land-Mine Triumph, a Crusade Against Small Arms
1998-01-07.abstract: Nongovernmental groups from academic institutes to human rights and aid agencies, encouraged by their surprising success in achieving treaty to ban land mines, are girding for campaign against assault rifles and other light weapons that inflame ethnic and nationalist wars around world; groups seek to curb brisk trade in light weapons that end up in hands of terrorist groups and organized crime syndicates as well as warring factions (M)

1998-01-07.title: A Wink and a Prayer: Netanyahu's Hope
1998-01-07.abstract: Defense Min Yitzhak Mordechai of Israel fails to attend scheduled meeting with Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu, prompting speculation they he might defect from Government; defection by Mordechai could topple Netanyahu's coalition, since resignation of Foreign Min David Levy and his five-man faction has left it with one-vote majority in Parliament (M)

1998-01-07.title: Despite Carnage, Algeria Shuns Aid Offers
1998-01-07.abstract: Algeria rejects all outside interference in its internal affairs as reports mount that Islamic militants are committing atrocities against Algerian civilians; Foreign Min Klaus Kinkel of Germany has called for dispatch of European Union mission to Algeria to help Government in dealing with crisis; Algerian newspaper La Tribune reports that 'several hundred people' were burned alive on Jan 4 in Relizane Province in western Algeria, where 412 people were reportedly slaughtered by Islamic rebels opposed to military-backed Government; map; photo (M)

1998-01-07.title: Reporter Visits Pol Pot, Still Held by Khmer Rouge
1998-01-07.abstract: Bangkok Post reports that Khmer Rouge guerrillas in Cambodia have allowed Thai reporter, Prasit Saengrungruang, to meet with their imprisoned former leader, Pol Pot, dispelling rumors that he had fled abroad from their jungle hideout; rumors have added to confusion in Cambodia as country begins political maneuvering before elections scheduled for July (M)

1998-01-07.title: Turkey's Top Court Weighs Move to Ban Islamic Party
1998-01-07.abstract: Ahmet Necdet Sezer takes over as chief of Turkey's Constitutional Court as it prepares to issue what may be its most important decision in country's modern history: case against Islamic-oriented Welfare Party; Federal prosecutor has urged that party be outlawed on grounds that it seeks to undermine secular basis of Turkish state (M)

1998-01-08.title: Mexican Governor Resigns in Aftermath of Indians' Massacre
1998-01-08.abstract: Julio Cesar Ruiz Ferro resigns as Governor of Chiapas, Mexico, in aftermath of killings of 45 Indian villagers by pro-Government paramilitary gang in Decembery; Pres Ernesto Zedillo names Rosario Green Macias to be Foreign Minister; she is believed to be highest-ranking woman ever in Mexican Cabinet (M)

1998-01-08.title: Seeking to Open a Door to U.S., Iranian Proposes Cultural Ties
1998-01-08.abstract: Pres Mohammad Khatami proposes cultural exchanges as way to end years of mistrust between Iran and US, but rules out Government-to-Government dialogue, saying there is no need for political ties with Washington; Khatami, in interview with Cable News Network, praises America's civilization, but he also attacks American policy towards Iran over last half century; he also repeats Iran's long-held opposition to Arab-Israeli peace talks, branding Israel 'racist terrorist regime'; photo (M)

1998-01-08.title: Unusual Naval Alliance Shows Off, and Arabs Glare
1998-01-08.abstract: Israel, Turkey and US conduct their first joint military exercise in Mediterranean Sea; Syria and Egypt denounce exercise as provocative and dangerous display of new naval dimension in budding strategic alliance between Israel and Turkey; photo (M)

1998-01-08.title: Russians Lament the Crime of Punishment
1998-01-08.abstract: Russian penitentiary system is arguably one of worst in world; prisons are underfinanced, overcrowded and unsanitary; human rights abuses abound; estimates suggest that tuberculosis rates in Russian prisons are anywhere from 20 to 60 times as high as in rest of population, which has TB death rate 24 times that of US; as many as 50 percent of Russian prisoners are believed to be infected; perhaps most terrifying aspect of Russian penal system is pretrial detention; close to 300,000 people awaiting trial are now in jail; unprotected from TB epidemic and other infectious diseases, many detainees end up spending two, three and even four years awaiting their day in court in packed cells; photos of inmates at Matrosskaya Tishina detention center in Moscow (M)

1998-01-08.title: A Priest Fights 'Defenders' Of His Faith In Bosnia
1998-01-08.abstract: Rev Ivan Mladina, Roman Catholic priest, says powerful Bosnian Croat political party wants to force out ethnic Croats who live in mostly Muslim area of Ovcari; Western officials say party's goal is to keep Croats in ethnically 'pure' enclaves where they can be controlled; it is notable that religious leader like Father Mladina is fighting political party because dominant parties in Bosnia justify their hard-line policies by claiming to protect their followers religions (M)

1998-01-09.title: Mexican Judge Accused; She Freed 5 in Slaying
1998-01-09.abstract: Dist Atty Samuel Del Villar of Mexico City accuses Judge Maria Claudia Campuzano of 'shocking carelessness' in her handling of case in which she released five men who had confessed to kidnapping and killing an American businessman, Peter Zarate (M)

1998-01-09.title: U.S. Mediator Seeking to Get Mideast Talks Restarted
1998-01-09.abstract: Dennis Ross, US mediator for Middle East, shuttles between Israeli and Palestinian politicians for third day in search of new formula to bridge widening gap between them and enable negotiations to resume; central question among Israelis is whether Government of Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu can survive any decision following resignation of Foreign Min David Levy (M)

1998-01-09.title: A Background Check Far From Ordinary
1998-01-09.abstract: Pakistan investigators say Asif Ali Zardari, husband of then Prime Min Benazir Bhutto, opened Citbank account in Geneva in Feb 1995 through which they say he passed some $40 million of the $100 million he received from payoffs from foreign companies doing business in Pakistan; bank declines comment (M)

1998-01-09.title: Kenyans Identify Virus Fatal to Hundreds
1998-01-09.abstract: Medical researchers say disease that has killed at least 300 people in northeastern Kenya is Rift Valley Fever and not deadly Ebola virus, as doctors had feared; fever has swept through dozens of remote villages in Kenya and Somalia that have been hit hard by flooding (M)

1998-01-09.title: An Opulent Estate, Quietly Bought in England's Countryside
1998-01-09.abstract: Asif Ali Zardari bought sprawling Rockwood estate in Brook, England, for $4 million in Oct 1995; Pakistani investigators say that in Rockwood deal, as in case of at least dozen other overseas properties they have linked to Bhutto family, elaborate steps were taken to disguise identity of estate's owners; photo (M)

1998-01-10.title: Albright Right-Hand Man Finds Spotlight's on Him
1998-01-10.abstract: Profile of James P Rubin, chief spokesman for State Department and right-hand man to Sec Madelein K Albright, in light of his engagement to Christiane Amanpour of CNN, one of the world's best-known reporters; photos (M)

1998-01-10.title: Beijing Journal; Delectable Materialism Catching On in China
1998-01-10.abstract: Popularity of newspapers like Shopping Guide and Fine Goods Shopping Guide attests to growth of consumer culture in China, at least among its younger generation; department stores and boutiques in Beijing carry brands like Benetton, Nautica and Sony, and ads on television and in magazines and some newspapers extol the world goods and gadgets; even People's Daily, Community Party mouthpiece, now reports on world of finance, shopping, society and culture; photo (M)

1998-01-10.title: Russia's Public Enemy No. 1 Takes Charge in Chechnya
1998-01-10.abstract: If all goes as planned, Saturday will qualify at least for a footnote in the long, garish history of Russian politics.    That's because sometime after noon a new government will take its place among the shards and rubble of Grozny, the Chechen capital.

1998-01-10.title: U.S. Aides Warm Up to Informal Iran Ties
1998-01-10.abstract: Senior US officials who were at first cool to Pres Mohammad Khatami's proposal for cultural exchanges between Iran and US begin to embrace the idea; acknowledge the courage of Khatami's address and constraints on what can be done in divided politics of theocratic Iran (M)

1998-01-10.title: Iranians Receptive To Opening To America
1998-01-10.abstract: Informal survey of Iranian citizens finds them supportive of Pres Mohammad Khatami's overture to American people; however, they differ widely in their interpretation of exactly what Khatami said in his television interview; photo (M)

1998-01-11.title: At W.H.O., 2 Physicians Lead the Race For Top Job
1998-01-11.abstract: World Health Organization, in most important election to take place in United Nations system since choice of new Secretary General in 1996, will replace its Director General in late Jan; Gro Harlem Brundtland, former Prime Minister of Norway, and Nafis Sadik of Pakistan, head of United Nations Population Fund, are among seven candidates for post; US has been critical of departing Dir Gen Hiroshi Nakajima for his personnel policies and his setting of priorities in health programs; he has led organization for 10 years (M)

1998-01-11.title: CONTRABAND WOMEN -- A special report.; Traffickers' New Cargo: Naive Slavic Women
1998-01-11.abstract: Selling of naive and desperate young women into sexual bondage has become one of fastest-growing criminal enterprises in robust global economy; international bazaar for women is hardly new; Asians have been its basic commodity for decades; economic hopelessness in Slavic world has opened what experts call most lucrative market of all to criminal gangs that have flourished since fall of Communism: white women with little to sustain them but their dreams; pimps, law enforcement officials and relief groups agree that Ukrainian and Russian women are now most valuable in trade; Israel says it has deported 1,500 Russian and Ukrainian women in past three years; for most, deportation offers only escape; photos (M)

1998-01-11.title: French Police Evict Jobless Protesters From Government Offices
1998-01-11.abstract: French Prime Min Lionel Jospin orders police across country to eject jobless protesters from public offices they have occupied since Dec to press demands for higher benefits (M)

1998-01-11.title: Turks Fear an Oil Disaster as the Bosporus Gets Busier
1998-01-11.abstract: Turkish officials say that tanker traffic in narrow Bosporus Strait could be reaching dangerous levels as huge amounts of oil and gas begin flowing from rich new fields in Caspian Sea basin; map; photo (M)

1998-01-11.title: Indonesia Opposition Chief Calls on President to Resign
1998-01-11.abstract: Indonesia's opposition leader Megawati Sukarnoputri calls on Pres Suharto to step down when his term ends in March and tells about 500 supporters that she is ready to succeed him; her declaration comes during period of growing public demands for end to Suharto's 32-year-rule as country's economy swirls in crisis; photo (M)

1998-01-12.title: A Major Hong Kong Bank Is Forced to Close Its Doors
1998-01-12.abstract: Peregrine Investments Holdings is forced to shut down, becoming first major casualty of the economic and currency crisis that has swept through Asia since summer and the first major bankruptcy in Hong Kong since territory reverted to Chinese rule (M)

1998-01-12.title: Kenichi Fukui, 79, Nobel Winner in Chemistry
1998-01-12.abstract: Dr Kenichi Fukui, Japanese theoretical and physical chemist who was one of two winners of the 1981 Nobel Prize for chemistry, dies at age 79; photo (S)

1998-01-12.title: Korean Banks Said to Be in Technical Default
1998-01-12.abstract: Moody's Investor Service says South Korea's banks are technically in default on billions of dollars of loans that have been twice extended by international lenders; Korean stocks surge 6.4 percent on Jan 11, as investors show growing optimism that nation's economy may rebound faster than others in Asia from financial crisis (M)

1998-01-12.title: U.S. Officials Tackle Crisis In Indonesia
1998-01-12.abstract: Team of Pres Clinton's top aides arrive in Singapore and immediately begins searching for ways to stop free fall of Asian currencies and stock markets; economic crisis in region is beginning to take a political and social toll, especially in Indonesia, where Pres Suharto is under pressure from three directions: the markets, International Monetary Fund and retired generals and academics calling for him to step aside or designate a successor whoc an soon take over; photo (M)

1998-01-12.title: Kinsman of Sinn Fein Chief Killed at Protestant Club
1998-01-12.abstract: Nephew-by-marriage of Gerry Adams, president of Sinn Fein, is shot dead outside a Belfast nightclub owned by David Ervine, leader of Progressive Unionist Party, which represents Protestant paramilitaries at formal peace talks that are set to begin; Ervine and Sinn Fein say killers are members of Loyalist Volunteer Force, a splinter group that separated itself from Ulster Volunteer Force, which later asserts its responsibility for killing; photos (M)

1998-01-13.title: With West's Help, Bosnian Serb President May Form Cabinet
1998-01-13.abstract: Bosnian Serb President, Biljana Plavsic, seems set to form new Government even though her candidates win only 15 of 83 seats in Parliament; Western supporters succeed in blocking backers of her rival, Radovan Karadzic, from obtaining parliamentary majority; Karadzic supporters hold 39 of 42 seats needed to form government (M)

1998-01-13.title: Asian Turmoil Putting Brakes On Arms Race
1998-01-13.abstract: Economic crisis in Asia is slowing military spending spree and throwing American, European and Russian arms makers into heated competition; big weapons buyers are abandoning some major arms deals with US and Russia and are postponing ambitious plans for others; Asia has been one of few growing markets for American-made weaponry; there is concern at Pentagon and among nation's military contractors that prolonged downturn could slash sales that Defense Department relies on to keep production costs lower for its own purchases; diagrams (M)

1998-01-13.title: Iraq Threatens to Stop Work Of a U.N. Inspection Team
1998-01-13.abstract: Iraq again threatens to block new UN arms inspection team because it has nine Americans and because it is led by Scott Ritter, former Marine captain who served in Persian Guf war (M)

1998-01-13.title: Suharto Vows to Step Up Economic Reform, IMF Says
1998-01-13.abstract: SINGAPORE—  President Suharto of Indonesia, under mounting international and domestic pressure to put into effect painful austerity measures or resign, agreed Monday to strengthen and accelerate the country's reform program, the International Monetary Fund said.

1998-01-13.title: Burdened by History, France Balks at Wading Into Algerian Strife
1998-01-13.abstract: France seems frozen into inaction in face of horrifying daily slaughter of Algeria, its former colony; French leaders of all political parties, paralyzed by their own equivalent of 'Vietnam syndrome,' are appalled by massacres of villagers, reportedly by Islamic militants, but agree that sending troops back into country would be folly (M)

1998-01-14.title: Iraq Bars Arms Inspectors Again, Saying American in Charge Is Spy
1998-01-14.abstract: Irag carries out its threat to block arms inspection by American-dominated team, provoking calls for action in Washington and foreign capitals, and forcing Security Council to look again for effective way to condemn Baghdad without threatening force; US officials reiterate earlier warnings but do not suggest what Council should do; Defense Sec William Cohen says Pres Saddam Hussein is not in position to dictate who can conduct inspections; photo of William Scott Ritter Jr, leader of barred inspectors (M)

1998-01-14.title: ISRAEL ANNOUNCES STRINGENT TERMS FOR WITHDRAWAL
1998-01-14.abstract: Israeli Cabinet says Israel will make no further withdrawal from West Bank unless Palestinians satisfy series of stringent conditions; Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat's aides dismiss conditions as another attempt by Israel to avoid any further withdrawal; it appears that Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu will go to Washington for meeting with Pres Clinton without any proposal that will enable stalled peace negotiations to resume; Clinton will meet with Netanyahu on Jan 20 and with Arafat on Jan 22; Israeli decision follows brief visit by US mediator Dennis Ross that was generally depicted as failure by Israelis and Palestinians (M)

1998-01-14.title: A Repentant France Honors Zola
1998-01-14.abstract: French Prime Min Lionel Jospin pays tribute to Emile Zola on 100th anniversary of publication of his manifesto against anti-Semitism of Dreyfus Affair (M)

1998-01-14.title: Blocking Money for U.N. Harms U.S. in Iraq Crisis, Albright Says
1998-01-14.abstract: Sec of State Madeleine K Albright accuses Congress of undermining US foreign policy during tense situation with Iraq late last year by failing to pay US debts to United Nations (M)

1998-01-14.title: Holocaust Survivor Presses Insurer for Annuity
1998-01-14.abstract: Allan Stern tells insurance commissioners of California and Washington that his uncle, Adolph, and other relatives who survived Holocaust tried for half century to get Assicurazioni Generali, Italy's biggest insurance company, to honor family policies worth estimated $1.5 million; commissioners are holding hearings in Los Angeles; they are part of committee of National Association of Insurance Commissioners, which is pressing Generali and others to pay thousands of similar claims (M)

1998-01-15.title: 'Q' for Quebec Electric Loses Political Glow, Too
1998-01-15.abstract: Week of heavy ice that wrecked electric pylons and left millions of people without power has cast shadow on image of Hydro-Quebec, government-owned company that is symbol of Quebec's self-sufficiency; number of households without power has been cut from peak of 1.4 million to 400,000; company's woes raise questions about policies that left Quebec one of most electricity-dependent places in world; photo (M)

1998-01-15.title: Chinese Dissident Refuses to Leave for Exile in the U.S.
1998-01-15.abstract: Democracy campaigner Qin Yongmin, 45, publicly defies State Security Ministry's demand that he leave China for exile in United States; sees Government reacting to his call for free labor unions, phone interview (M)

1998-01-15.title: Rio Journal; Death's New Sting in Brazil: Removal of Organs
1998-01-15.abstract: New law in Brazil makes all citizens over 21 potential organ donors unless they register objection on their official identity card; doctors do not have to notify relatives or ask permission to remove organs from dead for transplant; Brazilians caught unaware by poorly-publicized law are rushing to register their refusal; photo (M)

1998-01-15.title: An Iranian Says Gesture From U.S. Is Called For
1998-01-15.abstract: Reza Khatami, Pres Mohammad Khatami's adviser and brother, urges Clinton Administration to respond with gesture to brother's overture to American people, interview; overture gets both praise and criticism in Iran (M)

1998-01-15.title: Israel Sets Tight Limits for Any West Bank Pullback
1998-01-15.abstract: Israeli Government adopts sweeping guidelines defining its vital interests in any future talks on West Bank; cites security zones, Jerusalem and environs, military bases, Jewish settlements, roads and sacred sites; agrees on general principles, avoiding Cabinet showdown on specifics and giving Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu more room to maneuver in talks with Pres Clinton; photo (M)

1998-01-16.title: Palestinians Dismiss Terms Set by Israelis For a Pullout
1998-01-16.abstract: Palestinians reject conditions Israel has set for peace talks to continue and call on President Clinton to support them; tension is underscored in Gaza when Israeli and Palestinian troops aim rifles at each other during Palestinian protest over expansion of Jewish settlement (S)

1998-01-16.title: Arabs Want Israel to Feel U.S. Pressure
1998-01-16.abstract: Arab leaders make clear their hope that Pres Clinton will use upcoming meeting in Washington to take unparalled steps to persuade Israel to modify positions on next steps in Mideast peace efforts; Syrian Pres Hafez al-Assad is increasingly blunt in urging US to turn up heat on Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu; his unexpected talks with Egyptian Pres Hosni Mubarak are clear sign of Arab alarm (S)

1998-01-16.title: U.S. Gets Russia's Firm Vow To Halt Missile Aid to Iran
1998-01-16.abstract: US gets concrete promises of action by Russia to stop Russian companies and scientists from assisting Iran's ballistic missile program; US envoy Frank G Wisner Jr returns from Moscow and briefs Pres Clinton; Israel and Congress have put enormous pressure on Administration to halt such assistance (S)

1998-01-16.title: Vladimir Prelog, 91, Is Dead; Swiss Chemist Won the Nobel
1998-01-16.abstract: Vladimir Prelog, Swiss chemist whose pioneering insights into shape of complex biological molecules were honored with Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1975, dies at age 91; photo (M)

1998-01-16.title: Jerusalem Journal; Lost Thunder of Ramadan Echoes in the Old City
1998-01-16.abstract: Ramadan tradition of firing cannon to mark end of day's fast is carried out in Jerusalem, but with modern twist; fireworks are now set off by Rajal Sandouka, who says his family has been entrusted with duty for 80 years; Sandouka charges Israelis, concerned about security, have denied him gunpowder; Israeli authorities say Sandouka is free to use gunpower again, but must find his own supplier; photo of Muslim women at prayer (Jerusalem Journal) (S)

1998-01-17.title: U.S.-Led Arms Inspectors, Rebuffed by Iraq, Pull Out
1998-01-17.abstract: William Scott Ritter Jr, American weapons inspector at center of latest confrontation between Iraq and United Nations, leaves Baghdad with his inspection team, whose movements have been blocked since Jan 13 by Government of Pres Saddam Hussein; two other teams were allowed to carry out inspections; Britain sends aircraft carrier Invincible to Persian Gulf to join large US force; photo (M)

1998-01-17.title: Bonn to Let Police Tap Residences And Offices
1998-01-17.abstract: Lower house of German Parliament approves constitutional change that would give police greater powers to bug private homes in order to combat organized crime and money laundering; authorities are currently allowed to tap telephones only in exceptional circumstances relating to crime and terrorism and may use listening devices only to monitor emergencies like hostage-taking; move draws outcry from civil rights campaigners and from journalists, doctors and lawyers fearful that conversations with news sources, patients or clients can now be overheard; only priests, defense lawyers meeting accused criminals and legislators will be protected by law from eavesdropping (M)

1998-01-17.title: 2 Yeltsin Advisers Pushed More Deeply Into Shadows
1998-01-17.abstract: Russia's First Deputy Prime Minister, Anatoly Chubais, is removed as overseer of Government policy on news media, and its other First Deputy Prime Minister, Boris Nemtsov, loses responsibility for fuel and energy sectors; their free-market policies have made them both unpopular with some business tycoons (M)

1998-01-17.title: Liberia Waits: Which Charles Taylor Won?
1998-01-17.abstract: Liberian people are still waiting to see whether Pres Charles G Taylor will govern as a bullying former warload with an all-consuming ego or as an inspired modernizers who will make good on repeated pledges to turn country into a land that other African nations can look up to; his first six months as President has yielded evidence of both styles; photo; map (M)

1998-01-17.title: U.S. Can't Find Buyer for Jets Thailand Can No Longer Afford
1998-01-17.abstract: Thailand is trying to back out of a contract to buy eight F/A-18 fighter jets, whose $392 million cost is now beyond its means; US is in a bind because planes are incompatible with its arsenal and it cannot find another buyer in time for Thailand to keep its contract with plane's manufacturer, Boeing; Boeing, noting that Thailand technically purchased planes directly from US, not from it, regards matter as one between the two governments (M)

1998-01-18.title: Feast Is a Power Meal for the Family of Iran's President
1998-01-18.abstract: Three dozen women and children, all relatives of Iranian Pres Mohammad Khatami, gather in Yazd for nightly ritual of breaking a 12-hour fast that devout Iranian Muslims observe during holy month of Ramadan; map; photo (M)

1998-01-18.title: Tobacco Industry, Conciliatory in U.S., Goes on the Attack in the Third World
1998-01-18.abstract: Tobacco companies have agreed to sweeping restrictions in US on cigarette marketing and second-hand smoke and to bolder cancer-warning labels, but they are fighting as hard as ever in third world to convince media, public and policy makers that similar changes are not needed (M)

1998-01-18.title: Syria Warns U.S. It Must Act to Rescue Mideast Peace Effort
1998-01-18.abstract: Syrian Foreign Min Farouk al-Sharaa says quest for broader peace in Middle East has reached 'a very critical moment'; says US credibility could plummet across region unless Pres Clinton can use upcoming meetings with Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat and Israeli Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu to put effort 'back on the right track' (M)

1998-01-18.title: Argentines Fight Razing Of a Place Of Horrors
1998-01-18.abstract: Argentine Pres Calos Saul Menem has announced plans to demolish Navy Mechanics School in Buenos Aires where estimated 4,000 political prisoners were killed as part of military's 'dirty war' of repression against leftist guerrillas that ended 15 years ago; human rights groups, opposition political leaders, survivors of detention center and relatives of people who perished there oppose razing school and are calling for buildings to be preserved as museum (M)

1998-01-18.title: Turkish Ban of Islamic Party Won't Deter It, Leader Says
1998-01-18.abstract: Turkey's highest court bans Islamic-oriented Welfare Party and rules that its leader, former Prime Min Necmettin Erbakan, cannot participate in politics for next five years; Erbakan tells his followers that he will not abide by ruling, but younger members of party want to push him aside (M)

1998-01-19.title: Dejected Belgrade Embraces Hedonism, but Still, Life Is No Cabaret
1998-01-19.abstract: Mood of hedonism, bred by hopelessness and apathy, sweeps over Serbian capital of Belgrade; social collapse comes as inflation eats away at local currency, as infighting destroys political opposition and as criminal underclass continues its steady growth; effects of social collapse has devastating effect on children, who have been exposed to scenes of graphic sadomasochism on television; domestic violence, often by men who are out of work or have not received small salaries for months, appears widespread, and crime is endemic; photo (M)

1998-01-19.title: Reporter's Notebook; Indonesia a Bargain Now, Except for Most Indonesians
1998-01-19.abstract: Indonesia's economic crisis made the dollar king again after a decade in which cost of doing business throughout Asia's capitals skyrocketed for Americans; dollar's strength benefits mostly expatriates, many of whom are paid in dollars, but is useless for everyone else (M)

1998-01-19.title: John Paul Creates 22 More Cardinals, Including 2 American Archbishops
1998-01-19.abstract: Pope John Paul appoints 22 new cardinals, including Archbishop Francis Eugene George of Chicago and James Francis Stafford, Archbishop Emeritus of Denver and president of Pontifical Council for the Laity in Rome; of current 123 cardinals eligible to elect new pope, 106 have now been chosen by John Paul (M)

1998-01-19.title: Israel to Set No Pullback Goal Before Talks With Clinton
1998-01-19.abstract: Israeli Cabinet decides against setting figure for percentage of land to be included in next Israeli withdrawal from West Bank in advance of Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu's January 20 meeting with Pres Clinton in Washington; says it will later consider pullback up to unspecified ceiling; decision appears to be effort to free Netanyahu from trying to close gap between withdrawal that right-wing Cabinet would accept and pullback that US and Palestinians would consider satisfactory (M)

1998-01-19.title: Cuba and the Church Continue To Maneuver Over Pope's Visit
1998-01-19.abstract: Cuban Government and Roman Catholic Church are maneuvering over visit by Pope John Paul II that could determine whether political impact of trip is muted or enhanced; Pres Fidel Castro is seeking to use state-run media to portray Pope as ally in struggle against American imperialism, but his Government has erected obstacles making it difficult for church authorities to take their case to Cuban people; Castro reportedly believe he can direct packaging and presentation of Pope's visit in direction that suits his political goals (M)

1998-01-20.title: Gerry Adams, Meeting Blair, Objects to New Ulster Blueprint
1998-01-20.abstract: Gerry Adams and a delegation from his Sinn Fein party voiced their strong objections today to a new British-Irish negotiating blueprint in a ''frank'' meeting with Prime Minister Tony Blair. But they said they remained committed to participation in the current peace talks in Belfast.    They also said they would not be driven from the negotiations by the actions of renegade sectarian groups that have killed six people since Christmas and have promised more violence to drive home their objections to the talks and cease-fires being observed by the larger paramilitary groups.

1998-01-20.title: U.N. Envoy, In Baghdad, Hints Teams Could Alter
1998-01-20.abstract: The United Nations chief arms inspector for Iraq, Richard Butler, said here today that he might at some point be willing to take a second look at the composition of inspection teams, some of which the Iraqis have complained have too many Americans.    ''We choose the best that we can,'' he said, adding that experts were recruited from around the world to form teams tailored to certain sites and problems. ''Maybe in the future, we'll make a double take and look at their nationalities. But only after we look at their qualifications.''

1998-01-20.title: Mexico City Journal; News (and State) Anchor Weighs His
1998-01-20.abstract: After the Virgin of Guadalupe, Mexico's revered patroness, probably the most familiar image in this country is the impassive, bespectacled face of the television newscaster Jacobo Zabludovsky.    For 27 years Mr. Zabludovsky has been the anchorman of the weeknight prime-time news program on Televisa, which for decades was Mexico's only national network and remains its largest. For much of his career, Mr. Zabludovsky defined what most Mexicans would -- and would not -- know about what was happening in their country.

1998-01-20.title: Netanyahu, in U.S., Woos Conservatives
1998-01-20.abstract: The Israeli Prime Minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, arrived here today and went directly to a Christian-Jewish rally of conservative supporters, who include some of President Clinton's harshest political opponents.    While Mr. Clinton spoke today of his ''high hopes'' for the discussions about peace in the Middle East that are to begin on Tuesday, Mr. Netanyahu entered a ballroom at the Mayflower Hotel to thunderous applause in the middle of a speech by the Rev. Jerry Falwell, the founder of Moral Majority.

1998-01-20.title: Panel Backs Mexico Judge Who Freed 5 In Death Case
1998-01-20.abstract: An appeals tribunal today admonished a judge who described a murder defendant as a ''modern Robin Hood'' to express herself more carefully, but upheld her decision to free the man and four fellow suspects, who prosecutors say admitted killing an American executive.    The ruling on Jan. 1 by Judge Maria Claudia Campuzano in Mexico City prompted expressions of outrage from the United States Embassy as well as from the capital's new District Attorney.

1998-01-21.title: Zevulun Hammer, 61, Dies; Israel's Education Minister
1998-01-21.abstract: Zevulun Hammer, Israel's Education Minister, leader of National Religious Party and founder of movement to build Jewish settlements in occupied Arab territories, dies at age 61; photo (M)

1998-01-21.title: Museum Invites Arafat, Reversing Itself Twice
1998-01-21.abstract: United States Holocaust Memorial Museum again reverses stand and invites Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat to visit during his Washington trip; chairman Miles Lerman says he erred in handling invitation and is ready to act as Arafat's host and guide; Arafat, in Paris, expresses interest in visit (M)

1998-01-21.title: Evangelicals for Israel
1998-01-21.abstract: The Rev. Jerry Falwell said he and Southern Baptist leaders would urge evangelical churches to oppose the transfer of land to Palestinians.    Article, page A6.

1998-01-21.title: OBITUARY : Peter Diamand, 84, Music Administrator
1998-01-21.abstract: PARIS—  Peter Diamand, 84, former director of the Holland and Edinburgh music festivals and artistic adviser to the Orchestre de Paris since 1976, died Friday in Doetinchem, the Netherlands.

1998-01-21.title: With Pope Due, the Cubans Wrest Dollars From Heaven
1998-01-21.abstract: Television army has invaded Havana on eve of Pope John Paul II's visit to Cuba, speeding up city's normally slow-paced routine and probing without interference into nearly every corner of normally restrictive society; influx yields financial windfall for Government, particularly ministries and state companies providing services at greatly inflated prices and imposing creative new charges on equipment and supplies; photo (M)

1998-01-22.title: Iraq Orders Freeze on Arms Inspections
1998-01-22.abstract: Iraqi Pres Saddam Hussein calls for freeze on all weapons inspections and even discussion of sites considered to be sensitive; Deputy Prime Min Tariq Aziz says talks should be suspended until Apr, by which time Iraq should be close to having sanctions lifted and issue would be nearly moot, news conference; United Nations chief arms inspector Richard Butler rejects call, news conference before leaving Iraq; says he told Aziz that Security Council requires unconditional access to sites; says technical meetings to review progress on ending Iraq's output of special missile warheads for biological and chemical weapons and its nerve-gas program will begin Feb 1 (M)

1998-01-22.title: Clinton's Warning to Iraqis: Time for Diplomacy May End
1998-01-22.abstract: Pres Clinton warns time for diplomacy may be dwindling in standoff over Iraqi Pres Saddam Hussein's refusal to open suspected biological and chemical weapon sites to United Nations inspectors, television interview; suggest United States might take military action with or without support of allies or Security Council (M)

1998-01-22.title: I.R.A., in Veiled Threat, Says Proposals Throw Talks Into a Crisis
1998-01-22.abstract: Irish Republican Army, adding to tensions pervading peace talks, warns of crisis in Northern Ireland peace effort; analysts see veiled threat to resume terrorist campaign; statement comes shortly before Catholic man is shot dead in Belfast; British Min Paul Murphy scores apparent sectarian political killing (M)

1998-01-22.title: For the Exiles, Come Home, It's 'Miracle Time' in Havana
1998-01-22.abstract: Hundreds of Cuban-American pilgrims return to Cuba for Pope John Paul II's visit in largest mass return of exiles in nation's history; many struggle to reconnect to nation and people divided since Fidel Castrol seized power in 1959; photos (M)

1998-01-22.title: Castro's Spin: Pope Is on Our Side
1998-01-22.abstract: Analysis of Pres Fidel Castro's effort to convince Cubans that he and revolution he leads will benefit from Pope John Paul II's visit, which looks to rest of world like challenge to Castro; Castro has been so adept at co-opting visit that most Cubans think he sponsors it; photo (M)

1998-01-23.title: Cairo Journal; Fasting for Faith, This Winter, Is a Long Banquet
1998-01-23.abstract: Egyptians say Ramadan, Muslim holy month, has had special aura of charity and community this year; photo of poor people being served free meals in Cairo (M)

1998-01-23.title: Crowd Quiet And Staid: 'Pope Leaves, But We Stay'
1998-01-23.abstract: Most of signs that were put up in Santa Clara, Cuba, to welcome Pope John Paul II are taken down before he leaves provincial capital; in their place, posters bearing old revolutionary slogans of Pres Fidel Castro and Che Guevara have re-emerged (M)

1998-01-23.title: For Greeks in Turkey, Distrust Turns to Fear
1998-01-23.abstract: Arson attack and killing at holy Eastern Orthodox site in Istanbul on Jan 12, second violent assault there in two months, have led senior Orthodox figures to question Turkey's commitment to protect their church (M)

1998-01-23.title: Judge Denounces U.S. Visa Policies Based on Race or Looks
1998-01-23.abstract: Visa denials of hundreds of Brazilians from 1992 to 1994 are at heart of court battle in Washington, DC, that has left Federal District Judge Stanley Sporkin questioning whether State Dept is illegally refusing to issue visas to foreigners solely because of their skin color, their ethnic background, or way they look; Sporkin, in recent decision, said visa policies in Sao Paulo were illegal, ruling that could have far broader implications for State Dept since similar policies are in effect at US visa offices around world; ruling is victory for Robert E Olsen, visa officer in consulate who was dismissed by State Dept in 1993 on charges that he had failed to carry out his duties (M)

1998-01-23.title: Fraying Alliance: Iraq Sees Sanction Backers Divided
1998-01-23.abstract: Barbara Crossette analysis contends that Pres Saddam Hussein, in orchestrated series of steps, has sent world unmistakable message: seven years after Persian Gulf war, Iraq is going to extricate itself from economic sanctions, with or without permission from United Nations; maintains that next two or three months will be most critical tests of US, British and United Nations resolve that Iraq has posed since it was put under international supervision in Aug 1990 after it invaded Kuwait (M)

1998-01-24.title: Scientists Find No Evidence Of Bird Flu in Southern China
1998-01-24.abstract: Scientists find no evidence of bird flu in southern China, suspected source of bird flu that has killed six people in Hong Kong; Hong Kong's ban on imports of chickens from China will be lifted on Feb 7 (M)

1998-01-24.title: Venerable Brands Go Modern With Help From U.S. : Dusting Off the British Classics
1998-01-24.abstract: LONDON—  They never show on the runways. The fashion revolution of the 1980s passed them by. Their sales, led by classic English men's tailoring and coats, have ticked over pleasantly but unspectacularly.

1998-01-24.title: Arafat Departs, Calling Israeli Offer 'Peanuts'
1998-01-24.abstract: Yasir Arafat, Palestinian leader, leaves Washington considering new American compromises but rejecting Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu's offer of a limited Israel redeployment from less than 10 percent of the West Bank; officials of all sides say gaps between Israelis and Palestinians are narrowing (M)

1998-01-24.title: Pope's Call for Clemency Lifts Dissidents' Hopes
1998-01-24.abstract: Dissident movement in Cuba is cheered by Pope John Paul II's appeal for clemency for imprisoned members of human rights and political groups; case that has drawn most attention in recent months is that of Internal Dissidence Working Group, whose four principal leaders--Vladimiro Roca, Marta Beatriz Roque, Felix Bonne and Rene Gomez Manzano--were arrested in July; another important case is that of Hector Palacios Ruiz, leader of Democatic Solidarity; many of those arrested in last two years belong to Concilio Cubano, umbrella organization seeking to bring greater unity and coherence to dissident movement (M)

1998-01-24.title: Pope Urges Cubans to Find Their Christian Roots Again
1998-01-24.abstract: Pope John Paul II addresses Mass for young people in Camaguey, Cuba, urging Cuban people to find their Christian roots and resist a slide toward a life of 'emptiness,' without faith, morals or ideals; Camaguey, capital of Cuba's sugar cane-growing region, is consideed the most Catholic of Cuban cities, and the welcome for Pope there is warmer than he received in Santa Clara, where many seemed more puzzled than moved by Pope's presence; Gramma, Cuban Community Party newspaper, suggests that Government is willing to allow the church to expand its activities, one of the goal's of the Pope's visit; Pope delivers his first public critique of US embargo of Cuba, but in the context of a call to Cuban people to take control of their fate; photos; map (M)

1998-01-25.title: A West African Border With Back-to-Back Wars
1998-01-25.abstract: Bo Waterside, border area between Liberia and Sierra Leone, has become training ground for force, aided by Nigerian peacekeepers, that is seeking to oust Sierra Leone's military rulers; Nigerians, ostensible peacekeepers in regional effort to end years of fighting in Liberia, are reportedly arming and training fighters in Sierra Leone in effort to restore democratically elected president, Ahmad Tejan Kabbah; photos; map (M)

1998-01-25.title: Italy's New Fiscal Rigor Fails to Reassure Europe
1998-01-25.abstract: Doubts persist among Italy's partners in European Union despite success of center-left Government in more than halving budget deficit to 2.7 percent of gross domestic product--below limit set by Maastricht Treaty for members to join planned single currency in 1999; politicians in Netherlands and Germany are questioning Italy's ability to sustain new-found fiscal rectitude; key concern is country's public debt ratio, which at approximately 122.7 percent of gross domestic product is more than twice 60 percent goal set by Maastricht Treaty guidelines (M)

1998-01-25.title: A South Korean's Rise to Free Market Evangelist
1998-01-25.abstract: Article profiles You Jong Keun, governor of North Cholla Province in South Korea and chief economic adviser to Pres-elect Kim Dae Jung; photo; map (M)

1998-01-25.title: Mexico Frees 300 Prisoners To Seek Talks With Rebels
1998-01-25.abstract: Government of Mexican state of Chiapas frees 300 prisoners in what offficials call show of political will to help re-open peace talks with Zapatista rebels; prisoners are mostly Indian farmers serving sentences for minor crimes, and many are from highlands region where Zapatista National Liberation Army has many followers (M)

1998-01-25.title: Iran Keeps Its Distance On U.S. Crisis With Iraq
1998-01-25.abstract: Iran's refusal to show any sign of solidarity with Iraq's refusal to cooperate with UN weapons inspectors is seen as sign that Government has no intention of taking sides with longtime foe, risking criticism abroad; Pres Mohammad Khatami's refusal to meet with Iraq's Foreign Min Mohammed Said al-Sahaf during weeklong visit has been embarrassing snub by President who normally meets all high-ranking officials (M)

1998-01-26.title: Countdown on Iraq: U.S. Weighs February Attack
1998-01-26.abstract: Senior American and Western diplomats say US and Britain are laying plans for three- or four-day bombing campaign against Iraq as early as mid-February if Pres Saddam Hussein continues to block work of UN weapons inspectors; American officials vehemently insist in private that planning process on Iraq has not been altered by sex scandal involving Pres Clinton, but senior Western diplomats say it has already had impact on allied thinking, complicating Washington's options and giving allies pause; say scandal has emboldened Pres Hussein, noting Deputy Prime Min Tarqi Aziz has become even less flexible than before in private conversations with diplomats (M)

1998-01-26.title: Indonesian Stocks Rise as Rupiah Falls
1998-01-26.abstract: Indonesian stocks rise on January 26 as investors take advantage of difference between value of shares in Jakarta and New York as value of rupiah continues to plunge (S)

1998-01-26.title: The Pious and the Curious Share a Historic Moment
1998-01-26.abstract: Reporter's Notebook vignettes of events in Havana, Cuba, marking visit of Pope John Paul II; photo (M)

1998-01-26.title: Turkish Inquiry Links Government Agents to Assassinations
1998-01-26.abstract: Official investigating Turkey's bgiiest modern political scandal implicates previous Governmentts in clandestine murders and other illegal activities; 120-page report says Government spent millions of dollars on efforts to assassinate Kurdish rebels and overthrow Government of Azerbaijan (M)

1998-01-26.title: London Journal; Scare or No, the Britons Are Still Mad About Beef
1998-01-26.abstract: Many Britons have flouted law banning sale of beef on the bone because of outbreak of mad cow disease; while many butchers have stopped selling it for fear of arrest and prosecution, others pretend to have stopped, but are willing to slip contraband beef to special customers; photo (M)

1998-01-27.title: A Look at Trimming Aid to Israel by U.S.
1998-01-27.abstract: The Israeli Finance Minister will meet with Congressional and Administrative officials this week to discuss phasing out America's $1.2 billion in economic aid to Israel starting in the year 2000, according to Congressional officials and Jewish organizations.

1998-01-27.title: Small Group Of Protestants Quits Talks On Ulster
1998-01-27.abstract: Ulster Democratic Party, small Protestant party with links to paramilitary Ulster Freedom Fighters that admitted killing three Catholics earlier in month, quits Northern Ireland peace talks after pressure by other participants; departure is seen as another blow to already fragile peace effort (M)

1998-01-27.title: Yeltsin Sends Envoy to Iraq to Try to Finesse Crisis With the U.S.
1998-01-27.abstract: Russian Pres Boris Yeltsin, angered by what he considers needless intensification of tensions between Iraq and US, sends special envoy to Baghdad with orders to end mounting crisis over weapons inspections (M)

1998-01-27.title: Aksum Journal; Found in Ethiopia: Keepers of the Lost Ark
1998-01-27.abstract: Christian priests in Aksum, Ethiopia, claim to possess original Ark of the Covenant, most sacred object of Old Testament that rest of world has long believed is lost; say it has lain in country's most sacred city since time of Solomon, hidden among hundreds of other relics and old manuscripts; claim white stone tablet inscribed with Ten Commandments was carried down Mount Sinai by Moses; Biblical scholars say Ark appears to have been lost sometime between 995 BC, when Solomon built First Temple in Jerusalem to house it, and 557 BC, when Nebuchadnezzar's army razed city but reportedly failed to find it; legend of its arrival in Ethiopia is woven into fabric of country's national identity and lies at heart of its Christian tradition, which is heavily influenced by Old Testament; replicas remain at center of most Ethiopian Orthodox ceremonies, and are believed to contain spirit of God; photos; map (L)

1998-01-27.title: 'Who's a Jew' Puzzle Gets More Tangled
1998-01-27.abstract: Bitter dispute in Israel over who can perform conversionsto Judaism deepens with competing proposals before Chief Rabbis of Israel; during past seven months, committee headed by Finance Min Yaaakov Neeman has worked out compromse plan that would involve all three movements in preparing candidates for conversion, but actual conversions would still be done by Chief rabbinate, which is Orthodox; in Israel, only legal conversions are those performed by Orthodox rabbis; issue is important because with authority to decide who is Jew also comes power to decide who is eligible for automatic Israeli citizenship (M)

1998-01-28.title: Iraqis Must End Defiance On Arms, Clinton Warns
1998-01-28.abstract: Pres Clinton steps up pressure on Iraq, sending senior officials to consult with allies in Europe and Middle East and warning Pres Saddam Hussein, in State of Union address, that his defiance of United Nations arms inspections cannot continue; notes Hussein's past use of weapons of mass destruction and cites determination to deny him capacity to use them again; holds Congress is united on issue; weighs sending Defense Sec William S Cohen to Persian Gulf to seek backing for military strike; is sending State Sec Madeleine K Albright to Europe to confer with British, French and Russian counterparts on Iraq; Britian leads talks on new Security Council resolution to give Hussein another chance to back down; Russians seek way to avoid military strike (L)

1998-01-28.title: Uzbek Leader Dampens Signs of Islamic Fervor
1998-01-28.abstract: Uzbekistan Pres Islam Karimov, seeking to contain displays of Islamic piety, has conducted monthlong crackdown that local Muslim leaders say might spur radicalism he fears; police have removed mosque loudspeakers, detained hundreds of people, set curfews in two cities and harassed bearded men; Muslim republic has prided self on being island of secular stability in violent region; map (M)

1998-01-28.title: World News Briefs; Brazil Delays Vote On Environmental Bill
1998-01-28.abstract: Brazilian Congress defers vote on bill to give nine-year old environmental agency power to enforce environmental protection laws; environmentalist charge bill has become too diluted in political bargaining (S)

1998-01-28.title: Flagship Stadium:Echo of the Ancient World : Stade de France Is Set for World Cup Debut
1998-01-28.abstract: SAINT DENIS, France—  Gleaming with lights on a winter's day at dusk, the gigantic national stadium that France has built for the 1998 World Cup soccer tournament looks like an ocean liner about to glide out to sea.

1998-01-28.title: Ex-Prime Minister of Norway Wins Top Job at W.H.O.
1998-01-28.abstract: Former Norwegian Prime Min Gro Harlem Brundtland is elected World Health Organization director general, first woman to lead troubled agency; succeeds Dr Hiroshi Nakajima; pledges reform, interview and news conference; photo (M)

1998-01-29.title: Israel Sketches Out an Overall Drop in U.S. Aid Over 10 Years
1998-01-29.abstract: Israeli Finance Min Yaakov Neeman meets with Clinton Administration and Congressional officials to outline proposal by Israeli Government to phase out $1.2 billion in annual economic aid from US in exchange for $600 million increase in Washington's yearly military aid package (M)

1998-01-29.title: Brazil, Its Forests Besieged, Adds Teeth to Environmental Laws
1998-01-29.abstract: Brazilian Congress votes to give federal environmental agency legal authority to enforce environmental protection laws, after Government figures show destruction of Amazon rain forest reached record levels in mid-1990's (M)

1998-01-29.title: Baghdad May Ask Court in Hague to Intervene in Inspection Crisis
1998-01-29.abstract: Iraqi Foreign Min Mohammed Said al-Sahaf blames US and United Nations disarmament chief Richard Butler for creating 'fabricated crisis' over arms inspections; says Iraq may ask International Court of Justice to intervene in confrontation (M)

1998-01-29.title: Cuba's Protestant Churches: A Growing Flock
1998-01-29.abstract: Visit of Pope John Paul II to Cuba focused world's spotlight on budding revival of Catholicism there; religious experts say that revival has been dwarfed by explosive enthusiasm for Protestant churches, particularly for evangelical churches, that has swept country in recent years; Cuban Council of Churches says Protestant congregations, small religious minority before Fidel Castro seized power in 1959, have nearly doubled in number over last six years from some 900 to about 1,700; photo (M)

1998-01-29.title: Meciar Dominates Slovak Election
1998-01-29.abstract: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia—  The red neon numbers glaring down from a Bratislava building near the gracious 17th-century presidential palace are a rude reminder to Slovakia's president, Michal Kovac.

1998-01-30.title: Zambia Gazes at the Power Elite's Dirty Laundry
1998-01-30.abstract: Former Zambian Pres Kenneth D Kaunda is under house arrest while police try to build case that he was behind three-hour coup attempt on Oct 28; his lawyers are fighting to have him released, since prosecutors have presented no evidence, other than unsubstantiated statement that he agreed to give coup leaders money and jobs; map; photo (M)

1998-01-30.title: French Judge in War Crimes Case Said to Be Related to a Victim
1998-01-30.abstract: Trial of former French Vichy official Maurice Papon for complicity in German war crimes during occupation in World War II is mired in controversy about whether presiding judge, Jean-Lois Castagnede, should step down after unexpected assertion that he is distantly related to one of surviving victims (M)

1998-01-30.title: Will the Armed Forces Abandon Suharto After So Many Years?: In Indonesia, All Eyes on the Military
1998-01-30.abstract: SINGAPORE—  As Indonesia's economy stalls, social unrest and ethnic tension rise and the long rule of President Suharto draws to an end, the role of the armed forces in maintaining stability in the world's fourth-most-populous nation is becoming increasingly critical, analysts say.

1998-01-30.title: BRITAIN TO REOPEN ITS INQUIRY OF '72 IN ULSTER KILLINGS
1998-01-30.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair announces that he is opening new judicial inquiry into killings of 14 unarmed civil rights marchers by British soldiers in Northern Ireland on Jan 30, 1972: Blair's action amounts to reopening of widely disparaged investigation in 1972 that exonerated heavily armed paratroopers and faulted civilian protesters; Blair says he is setting up three-judge panel led by Lord Saville; killings, in Londonderry, fueled hatred of British Government and its Protestant supporters and provoked thousands of young men to join Irish Republican Army; all victims were Catholics; photo (M)

1998-01-30.title: Jailed Doctor's Wife Is Hoping the Pope's Words Sway Cuba
1998-01-30.abstract: Caridad Pinon Rodriguez hopes that Cuban Pres Fidel Castro will respond to Pope John Paul II's request to free political prisoners; her husband, Dr Dessi Mendoza, is human rights advocate who is serving eight-year sentence on charges of 'disseminating enemy propaganda' (M)

1998-01-31.title: STANDOFF WITH IRAQ: SPLENDOR AND RUIN -- A special report.; As Hussein Builds, His People Struggle to Live
1998-01-31.abstract: Special report, Splendor and Ruin, describes Saddam Hussein's massive construction projects that are being carried on even as Iraqi people suffer great deprivation from international embargo; stupendous new palace on small hill in Baghdad and ground-breaking for world's largest mosque are but two examples of Hussein's excess as his people plot how to pay next grocery bill or get medicine for life-threatening illnesses; photos; most Iraqis direct anger at US, at least in public, but are under constant scrutiny by vast network of security agents in most neighborhoods; Hussein rules by potent combination of terror and secrecy, and is rarely seen in public; economic collapse has gutted Iraqi intelligentsia, once one of most highly respected in Arab world, and destroyed middle class; professionals now earn about $2 per month, medicine ran out in 1994 and children die of curable diseases; land is part of ancient Fertile Crescent but modern Iraqis did not develop agriculture, relying on oil money and using energy with abandon even now; Iraqis now survive by selling possessions, or trying to emigrate (M)

1998-01-31.title: Backing U.S., France Warns Iraqis to Yield on Arms Inspections
1998-01-31.abstract: France reinforces American threats of military action by warning Iraq it faces very grave consequences unless Pres Saddam Hussein allows continued UN inspections of suspected chemical and biological weapons sites (M)

1998-01-31.title: Ethnic Violence Flares Anew After Election in Kenya
1998-01-31.abstract: Less than month after Pres Daniel arap Moi won re-election in contest that divided Kenyans along tribal lines, new round of tribal clashes has broken out in Rift Valley region near town of Sipili, claiming at least 80 lives in last two weeks; police assert killings are work of cattle rustlers, who have long operated in region, but opposition leaders and clerics say attacks appear to be well-orchestrated campaign to drive members of Kikuyu tribe from area, which is political stronghold of President; Kikuyu voted against Moi in election, overwhelmingly backing kinsman, Mwai Kibaki, economist and former Vice President; map; photo (M)

1998-01-31.title: Drug Lords Infiltrated a Mexico Inquiry Into Disappearances
1998-01-31.abstract: Government investigation into disappearance of scores of people in northern Mexico is thrown into turmoil by discovery that narcotics traffickers infiltrated unit carrying out probe; discovery follows January 18 gangland-style execution of Hector Mario Varela, Federal police officer with ties to traffickers who was assigned to participate in investigation; Mexican Atty Gen Jorge Madrazo comments (M)

1998-01-31.title: Washington And Moscow Show Split Over Iraq
1998-01-31.abstract: US and Russia display sharpest disagreements over Iraq, with Sec of State Madeleine K Albright expressing deep skepticism in Madrid, Spain, that diplomatic solution to standoff is possible, and Russian Foreign Min Yevgeny Primakov stating that negotiating process has just begun; combined with call by China for peaceful resolution to problem, Russia's stand is setback for Albright, who has been touring Middle East to win support from Arab countries (M)
